title: Context Engineering for Multi-Agent LLM Code Assistants Using Elicit, NotebookLM, ChatGPT, and Claude Code
abstract: The paper addresses challenges LLMs face with complex, multi-file projects due to context limitations and knowledge gaps. Proposes integrated workflow combining multiple AI tools: Intent Translator (GPT-5), Elicit (semantic literature retrieval), NotebookLM (document synthesis), and Claude Code's multi-agent system for generation and validation.
key_insights: Multi-component architecture: Intent Translator + Elicit + NotebookLM + Claude Code
Higher single-shot success rates vs baseline single-agent approaches
Context injection and agent role decomposition lead to SOTA performance
Effective planning, editing, testing with minimal human intervention

applications.title: Intent Translation Layer
applications.description: Improve requirement clarification before execution - ensure clear understanding of user intent
applications.technical_approach: Add intent clarification step before task execution, prompt for ambiguity resolution

applications.title: Agent Role Decomposition
applications.description: Clear role separation: Planner, Editor, Tester, Validator for better coordination
applications.technical_approach: Define distinct agent roles with specific responsibilities, implement handoff protocols

review_status.summary: Interesting multi-component approach but relies on external tools (Elicit, NotebookLM). Role decomposition insights are valuable. Medium priority - extract patterns rather than full implementation.
review_status.recommended_action: Reference for agent orchestration patterns. Low implementation priority.

implementation_status.notes: Extract patterns for role decomposition - full workflow too complex
